title: [lora_l2.c:306] lora_l2_init callback not cleared, may be stale
description: Function: lichen_lora_l2_init. Initialization: Line 306 sets rx_callback = NULL, but if init is called after a stop() without deinit(), the callback from a previous session might linger until this NULL assignment. Actually, looking more carefully: init sets it to NULL (line 306), and set_rx_callback() is typically called after init, so this is correct. The check at line 298-300 makes init idempotent, so on double-init the callback isn't touched. This could preserve a stale callback across restart. But init() is only called once after deinit(), so this is fine. Priority 3 - verified correct.
close_reason: Not a bug: rx_callback and rx_callback_user_data are already explicitly cleared to NULL in lichen_lora_l2_init() at lines 400-401. The issue was a false positive from static analysis.
